text.title: hybrid-search-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Three observable antipatterns when configuring hybrid search.

antipattern.description: Combining raw BM25 and cosine scores by addition or weighted sum without normalising.
antipattern.reason: BM25 is unbounded and corpus-size dependent; cosine is 0-1. The sum is dominated by BM25 magnitude noise. Detector: top-K becomes BM25-only over time. Fix: switch to RRF (r1-rrf-default) or min-max normalise per leg before linear fusion (r3-never-sum-raw).

antipattern.description: Using a single alpha=0.5 across all domains without per-domain tuning.
antipattern.reason: Optimal alpha varies by corpus type. Symptom: NDCG plateaus below per-domain best. Detector: NDCG sweep shows clear winner ≠ 0.5. Fix: tune alpha per domain on a labeled set (r2-tune-alpha) and ship the winner.

antipattern.description: Choosing fusion strategy or alpha "by feel" without running an alpha sweep against a labeled set.
antipattern.reason: Decisions become irreproducible and rollbacks lose context. Detector: config has no `eval` block. Fix: require `eval` block in config schema (r2-tune-alpha); reject configs without it.
